LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS ELDRIDGE JOE, III, AND WILLIAM KING'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION DENIED. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO DEMONSTRATE AN INADEQUATE REMEDY AT LAW. PLAINTIFFS' REMEDY IS TO SUE FOR DAMAGES IN AN EMPLOYMENT CASE. THIS IS NOT A CLASS ACTION AND PLAINTIFFS DO NOT HAVE REPRESENTATIONAL STANDING. PLAINTIFFS' DELAY IN BRINGING THIS ACTION SHOWS THAT THERE IS NO IMMINENT HARM. PLAINTIFFS FAIL TO SHOW A LIKELIHOOD OF PREVAILING ON THE MERITS. VEHICLE CODE SECTION 22502 (B) ALLOWS COMMERCIAL VEHICLES TO DOUBLE PARK WHEN IT "IS REASONABLY NECESSARY TO ACCOMPLISH THE LOADING AND UNLOADING." IT IS UNCLEAR THAT PLAINTIFFS' SUPERVISORS WERE TELLING PLAINTIFFS TO BREAK THE LAW. THE PROPOSED INJUNCTION IS OVERBROAD BECAUSE IT WOULD GIVE THE DRIVERS AUTHORITY TO REFUSE TO DOUBLE PARK IN ALL INSTANCES, EVEN WHEN IT WAS INDISPUTABLY LEGAL. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA LERTORA, CSR #11935
MINI-MINUTES FOR OCT-22-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC.'S MOTION FOR SANCTIONS (CODE CIV. PROC. SECTION 128.7). ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - MOTION DENIED ON GROUND PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE NOT WARRANTED BY EXISTING LAW. PLAINTIFF'S INTERPRETATION OF VC SECTION 22500 IS OBJECTIVELY REASONABLE. MOTION DENIED ON GROUND THAT COMPLAINT WAS FILED FOR AN IMPROPER PURPOSE. DEFENDANT FAILS TO ESTABLISH THAT THE COMPLAINT WAS FILED FOR THE PRIMARY PURPOSE OF HARASSING DEFENDANT. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) Plaintiffs Eldridge Joe, Iii And William King'S Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction (Per Stipulation & Order Of 09/23/09)
Off Calendar Oct-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiffs Eldridge Joe, III, and William King's Order to Show Cause re: preliminary Injunction. Appearances: David A. Rosenfeld, Esq. of Weinberg, Roger & Rosenfeld (510) 337-1001, appearing for Plaintiffs and moving party; Sandra Rappaport, Esq. and W. Daniel Clinton, Esq. of Hanson Bridgett appearing for Defendant UPS. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for preliminary injunction denied. Plaintiffs fail to demonstrate an inadequate remedy at law. Plaintiffs' remedy is to sue for damages in an employment case. This is not a class action and plaintiffs do not have representational standing. Plaintiffs' delay in bringing this action shows that there is no imminent harm. Plaintiffs fail to show a likelihood of prevailing on the merits. Vehicle Code section 22502 (b) allows commercial vehicles to double park when it "is reasonably necessary to accomplish the loading and unloading." It is unclear that plaintiffs' supervisors were telling plaintiffs to break the law. The proposed injunction is overbroad because it would give the drivers authority to refuse to double park in all instances, even when it was indisputably legal. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Maria Lertora, CSR #11935
